prompt: Read src/domains/dialogue/mod.rs and src/domains/dungeon/mod.rs. For EACH domain audit: (1) Does it import from crate::shared and use bounded types where appropriate? (2) Any new_unchecked on runtime values? (3) Dead code or unreachable branches? (4) Test coverage: do tests exercise the main paths or just trivial construction? (5) Contract violations: local type redefinitions, cross-domain imports? (6) Any hardcoded magic numbers that should be in data/tuning? Return JSON: {domain: string, loc: int, tests: int, bounded_type_usage: string, unchecked_risks: [{line: int, issue: string}], dead_code: [string], test_quality: string, contract_violations: [string], magic_numbers: [{line: int, value: string}], overall_grade: string}

Read src/domains/encounter/mod.rs and src/domains/menu/mod.rs. Same audit: (1) bounded types used? (2) unchecked risks? (3) dead code? (4) test quality? (5) contract violations? (6) magic numbers? Return JSON per domain with same schema.
Read src/domains/puzzle/mod.rs and src/domains/quest/mod.rs. Same audit schema. Check if puzzle mechanics are actually testable or just stubs. Check if quest state transitions are exhaustive.

Read src/domains/screens/mod.rs src/domains/shop/mod.rs src/domains/town/mod.rs. Same audit schema. Check if shop uses Gold bounded type for transactions. Check if town connects to world_map correctly.

Read src/domains/world_map/mod.rs src/game_loop.rs src/game_state.rs src/starter_data.rs. Same audit schema. game_loop is 1010 LOC — check for god-function patterns, missing error handling, unreachable states. starter_data: are values reasonable or placeholder? game_state: does it use bounded types?
